text: Napping between 20 to 90 minutes in duration can benefit athletes' physical performance, cognitive performance, and perceptual measures such as fatigue and alertness.
The optimal timing window for napping in athletes is between 13:00 and 16:00 hours.
Athletes should allow at least 30 minutes after waking from a nap to reduce sleep inertia before training or competition.
Daytime napping can supplement insufficient night-time sleep in athletes without compromising night-time sleep quality.
Napping may improve psychological state in athletes, in addition to physical and cognitive performance benefits.
